name: Richard Weaver & Associates
description_short: Dallas-based bankruptcy law firm offering Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filing, foreclosure defense, and debt relief services across the Dallas-Fort Worth area.
description_long:
Richard Weaver & Associates is a bankruptcy and debt relief law firm operating in the Dallas-Fort Worth region with multiple office locations including Downtown Dallas, Highland Park, Cedar Hill, Garland, Irving, Keller, and McKinney. The firm is led by Richard Weaver, a bankruptcy attorney focused on helping clients navigate financial hardship through legal remedies.

The firm provides comprehensive bankruptcy services including Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, emergency bankruptcy filings, and extensive debt relief options. Beyond bankruptcy, they offer foreclosure defense, deed-in-lieu of foreclosure alternatives, loan modifications, real estate short sales, and assistance with creditor harassment, wage garnishments, bank account freezes, and repossession. They also address specialized debt issues including credit card debt elimination, medical debt relief, and payday/title loan management.

Richard Weaver & Associates distinguishes itself through educational content challenging bankruptcy myths and emphasizing that bankruptcy is an ethical solution for hardworking people facing financial hardship. The firm emphasizes that most bankruptcy filers do not lose assets and that bankruptcy can actually improve credit scores immediately. They position experience as a key factor in choosing bankruptcy representation.

The firm's main limitation is that detailed information about attorney credentials, years in practice, specific case outcomes, fee structures, or client testimonials is not provided on the website. The scope of services is broad, but potential clients lack granular details about specialization or success rates that would help differentiate this firm from competitors.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Multiple convenient office locations across Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex (8 locations listed)
Offers both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy options with emergency filing services
Comprehensive debt relief services beyond bankruptcy, including foreclosure defense and creditor harassment mitigation
Educational focus debunking bankruptcy myths and addressing common client fears about asset loss
Addresses specialized situations like wage garnishments, bank account freezes, and repossession prevention
Provides post-bankruptcy credit rebuilding guidance and home purchase counseling
Covers real estate alternatives including short sales, deed-in-lieu, and loan modifications
cons: Website lacks specific attorney credentials, bar certifications, or years of experience information
No published fee structure, pricing models, or payment plan options disclosed
Absence of client testimonials, case results, or success rate metrics to evaluate firm quality
Limited detail on initial consultation process, timeline, or what to expect during representation
No information about bankruptcy trustee relationships or court-specific expertise
best_for: Dallas-Fort Worth area residents facing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy decisions
Homeowners in foreclosure seeking alternatives to losing their homes
Individuals experiencing wage garnishment, creditor harassment, or repossession threats
diagnosis: Best for North Texas residents actively considering bankruptcy or facing foreclosure/garnishment who value multiple office locations and comprehensive debt relief options beyond filing. Primary caveat: lack of published attorney credentials, fee transparency, and client testimonials makes it difficult to compare this firm against competitors without direct contact.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
